ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic waffle entry (FOODON_03315091); no cheese-filled waffle variant exists. Matched to the closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers waffles generically; no cheese-filled sub-variant code exists. Matched to the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cheese-filled waffle; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cheese waffle as sold at Singaporean street-food stalls or hawker centres, approximately 100 g.

nutrient_source.title: Waffle (cheese) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Waffle (cheese), cr_id F11100029, confidence 0.95. All nine reported nutrients are used as-is.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cheese-filled waffle in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 76 based on the closest analogous entry: plain waffle (GI ~76, University of Sydney GI Database). The cheese filling contributes protein and fat that may modestly lower the effective GI, but the dominant carbohydrate matrix (refined wheat flour, sugar) drives the high GI classification. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (76 × 43.63) / 100 = 33.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 295 mg/100 g combined with high sugar 15.47 g/100 g driving insulin-mediated sodium retention
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid cheese waffles due to the combined burden of 295 mg sodium per 100 g and 15.47 g sugar, which together promote fluid retention and elevated blood pressure; if consumed, restrict to a half-portion and pair with a low-sodium meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar 15.47 g/100 g with low dietary fibre 1.53 g/100 g and high GI (~76) producing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit cheese waffles because the high sugar content (15.47 g/100 g), minimal fibre (1.53 g/100 g), and estimated GI of 76 combine to produce a rapid and sustained postprandial glucose spike; if consumed, limit to a small portion and balance with a high-fibre, low-GI food.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100 g from cheese and butter, combined with high sugar and sodium, elevating LDL and blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit cheese waffles due to the estimated saturated fat content of approximately 7.5 g/100 g, sodium at 295 mg/100 g, and sugar at 15.47 g/100 g, which collectively raise LDL cholesterol, promote arterial inflammation, and increase blood pressure; occasional consumption of a small portion is preferable to regular intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100 g and sugar 15.47 g/100 g promoting pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit cheese waffles because the estimated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) and high sugar (15.47 g/100 g) promote systemic inflammation, while sodium at 295 mg/100 g may worsen joint-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheese waffle is a popular Singaporean street snack consisting of a crispy, golden waffle made from a sweetened pancake-style batter — typically containing wheat flour, eggs, milk, butter, and sugar — filled or layered with processed cheese. The waffle is cooked in a waffle iron until the exterior is crisp and lightly browned, while the interior remains soft and slightly chewy. The flavour profile combines sweet, buttery batter with the savoury, salty tang of melted cheese, creating a contrast that is characteristic of Singaporean hawker-stall snack culture. Per 100 g, the cheese waffle provides 345 kcal, 9.27 g protein, 14.9 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g from cheese and butter), 43.63 g carbohydrate, 15.47 g sugar, 1.53 g dietary fibre, 295 mg sodium, and 292 mg calcium. The high calcium content reflects the cheese component. Clinically, the cheese waffle is a high-concern food for individuals managing type 2 diabetes (high GI ~76, high sugar, low fibre), hypertension (elevated sodium and sugar), and cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and sodium). It is best consumed occasionally and in small portions by individuals with these conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a cheese waffle?
Is cheese waffle high in sodium?
What is the GI of a cheese waffle?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100029, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean foods. All nine reported nutrients are used as-is. Saturated fat is estimated from the known composition of cheese-and-butter waffles and is consistent with the total fat value. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain waffle, GI ~76).
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Waffle (cheese) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated_fat_g is estimated at 7.5 g/100 g based on typical cheese-and-butter waffle composition consistent with the total fat of 14.9 g/100 g; this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain waffle); no direct GI measurement for cheese-filled waffle was found. All core macros (protein_g 9.27, fat_g 14.9, carbohydrate_g 43.63) are non-zero and sourced from HPB.
